Deployed code changes for the 503 error to reduce Tidal failures.

Tidal is experiencing issues due to a ""503 Server Unavailable"" error. To minimize the occurrence of this error, I made some changes to Tidal to utilize the same resources for everyone at 20-minute intervals. Additionally, I found that sometimes rethrowing exceptions can crash the server and result in a 503 error from the server side. Therefore, I implemented exception handling within the same function instead of relying on the caller functions for exception handling.

@Vippin Parusha  In looking at the history of this job, it usually runs around 700 minutes.  To avoid receiving Long Running Job incidents, you can look through the history of this job to determine what you think the longest expected run time should be.  Once determined, you can set a maximum value on the job through a Standard Change (link below).  Once the maximum duration is set, the estimated duration will no longer be used by the long running job logic, and instead, the maximum duration will be used to create the long running incident once met.

For example, let's say every 3 days the job runs around 575 minutes.  I would suggest setting a max duration of 605 minutes to give a 30 minute ""buffer"" before the incident is generated.  If the job runs 1 minute past the maximum duration, an incident will be auto-generated, because you are essentially saying, ""this is the MAXIMUM time allotted for this job to run.""  There is no 30 minute buffer as there is when using the estimated duration, so that's why I suggest you ""build"" one in.
